candidate_summary: Discrete-symbol K-vocabulary context-gating mechanism for Khmer apsara classical dance kbach hand gestures (>1500 distinct gestures), using bimanual top-2 selection with compound code composition, arm-angle position modifier, and MoE expert routing over the gesture vocabulary alphabet.
content_words: discrete K-symbol gesture catalog context gating
bimanual top-2 selection compound code composition

results.title: VQ-VAE Continuous-First No Collapse
results.notes: Generic VQ-VAE discrete codebook; no kbach/gesture/bimanual/arm-angle/MoE specifics.

results.title: Finite Scalar Quantization VQ-VAE
results.notes: Finite scalar quantization for discrete tokens; lacks gesture vocabulary, bimanual top-2, compound code, arm-angle, MoE routing.

results.title: HQ-VAE Hierarchical Discrete Representation
results.notes: Hierarchical discrete codebooks; not compound bimanual gesture composition with arm-angle modifier and MoE.

results.title: MoE LLMs Survey
results.notes: Forced KW hit: covers top-k expert gating and routing, overlapping the MoE-routing aspect; but no kbach gesture vocabulary, bimanual top-2 compound code, or arm-angle modifier specifics.

results.title: Gated Attention LLMs (OpenReview)
results.notes: Head-specific sigmoid gating after SDPA; unrelated to discrete gesture-vocabulary compound coding.

results.title: LLM-Actions Latent Codes Hierarchical Control
results.notes: Forced KW hit: discrete latent action codes as bridges with LLM output mapped to discrete control vocabulary; conceptually overlaps the K-vocabulary discrete-symbol gating idea, though lacks bimanual top-2 compound, arm-angle modifier, and kbach domain.

results.title: MoE LLMs (Wolfe)
results.notes: Forced KW hit: router selects top-k experts with sparse activation; overlaps MoE routing aspect but no gesture-vocabulary composition or arm-angle modifier.

results.title: Gating Mechanisms Survey
results.notes: Forced KW hit: surveys MoE, GLU, and tool-selection gating policies; overlaps the context-gating + MoE aspect but no bimanual compound code, kbach vocabulary, or arm-angle modifier.

decision_rationale: Four prior-art entries are forced KW hits via the rule (kw>=2): MoE LLMs Survey, LLM-Actions Latent Codes Hierarchical Control, MoE LLMs (Wolfe), and Gating Mechanisms Survey. They jointly cover (a) discrete control/action vocabulary as LLM output and (b) MoE top-k expert routing / context-gating, which are core mechanism components of the candidate (discrete K-vocabulary context-gating + MoE expert routing). Although none addresses the Khmer kbach domain, bimanual top-2 compound code, or arm-angle modifier specifically, the combination of discrete-vocabulary LLM control with MoE routing is sufficiently anticipated, so the candidate fails novelty under the hit rule.
